summary: Retested Split Sleeper Practice tab presets after main agent's fixes for midnight-clamp bug and verdict text wording. All 4 presets now produce the expected verdict (color + wording). No NASI-A references on /hours-of-service or /hours-of-service/training.

test_results.preset-valid-8-2: PASS — GREEN verdict, text 'Legal 8+2 split detected — Valid 8+2 split — 8h SB + 2h OFF.'
test_results.preset-valid-7-3: PASS — GREEN verdict, text 'Legal 7+3 split detected — Valid 7+3 split — 7h SB + 3h OFF.'
test_results.preset-invalid-6-4: PASS — RED verdict 'No valid split' with proper feedback.
test_results.preset-invalid-5-5-sb: PASS — RED verdict 'No valid split' with proper feedback.

critical_code_review_comments: hosRules.validateSplit sorts by hours descending; with equal hours (e.g. 5/5 SB) the 'big' picked is arbitrary — currently correctly flagged invalid (big=5 fails 7+3/8+2). No fix required.

PracticeTab restCandidates uses block.hours directly which correctly ignores midnight-clamping; good fix. Edge case: if a user sets hours so block exceeds 24:00 start+hours, the visual EldGrid is clamped but the verdict still uses the nominal hours — acceptable for trainer UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both iteration_14 fixes verified on preview URL. hosRules.validateSplit returns type '8+2'/'7+3' and PracticeTab derives rest-candidate durations from raw block.hours (not entry end-start), so midnight-clamped SB blocks like 18:00+8h no longer mis-evaluate.
rca of the issue: Previously resolved by E1: (1) validateSplit label changed from '7/3'/'8/2' to '7+3'/'8+2'. (2) PracticeTab restCandidates now filters blocks using b.hours ≥ 2 before clamping, so 'Valid 8+2' preset (SB 18:00 for 8h) no longer loses 2h to 24:00 clamp. Both verified visually + via DOM text/class inspection.
